title: Carlos Ezquerra
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Person |image=[[File:CarlosEzquerra.jpg]] |name=Carlos Ezquerra |birth=[[November 12]], [[1940s#1947|1947]]
[[Wikipedia:Zaragoza|Zaragoza]], [[Wikipedia:Spain|Spain]] |death=[[October 1]], [[2018]] |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:Spain|Spanish]] |occupation=Artist |companies= |website= }} '''Carlos Ezquerra''' ([[November 12]], [[1940s#1947|1947]] – [[October 1]], [[2018]]), who has also worked under the alias ''L. John Silver'', was a Spanish comics artist who worked on the comic ''[[Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand]]''. He worked mainly in British comics and lived in Andorra. He was best known as the co-creator of [[Wikipedia:Judge Dredd|''Judge Dredd'']]. ==Biography== In the 1970s, he worked for Battle Picture Weekly, drawing such strips as ''Rat Pack'', ''Major Eazy'' and ''El Mestizo''. During the development of [[Wikipedia:2000 AD (comic)|''2000 AD'']], he created the visual designs for the characters, cityscapes, and technologies for writer John Wagner's character ''Judge Dredd''. Despite creating the character, Wagner and Ezquerra's original ''Judge Dredd'' strip was passed on by editor Pat Mills in favor of a more introductory story by another creative team for the character's debut, and their original strip would not be used until years later in an annual. Upset with management disputes, like his and Wagner's ''Judge Dredd'' story being passed on for someone else's work on a character he helped create, Ezquerra left ''2000 AD'' to return to work for ''Battle Picture Weekly'' in 1977. Carlos Ezquerra and John Wagner also created the character [[Wikipedia:Strontium Dog|''Strontium Dog'']] for [[Wikipedia:Starlord|Starlord]] in 1978; the strip would later transfer to ''2000 AD'' when ''Starlord'' folded. Other ''2000 AD'' strips he drew included [[Wikipedia:Fiends of the Eastern Front|''Fiends of the Eastern Front'']] and adaptations of Harry Harrison's [[Wikipedia:Stainless Steel Rat|''Stainless Steel Rat'']] novels. He would later return to ''Judge Dredd'' to draw one of the most infamous mega epics, the [[Wikipedia:Apocalypse War|''Apocalypse War'']], and would go on to draw some of his most memorable adventures. Always a fan favorite, Ezquerra still draws ''Judge Dredd'' and ''Strontium Dog'' from time to time. Ezquerra also collaborated numerous times with writer Garth Ennis on ''[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloody_Mary_(comics) Bloody Mary]'', [[Wikipedia:Adventures in the Rifle Brigade|''Adventures in the Rifle Brigade'']], ''[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_Stories_(comics) War Stories]'', a ''[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hitman_(comics) Hitman]'' annual with artist Steve Pugh, and two ''[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Preacher_(comics) Preacher]'' specials (''The Good Old Boys'' and ''The Saint of Killers'' miniseries) for DC Comics, and [[Wikipedia:Just a Pilgrim|''Just a Pilgrim'']] for Black Bull Entertainment. He provided the art for the [[Wikipedia:Judge Dredd|''Judge Dredd'']] epic ''Origins'' in ''2000 AD''. Carlos passed away from lung cancer on October 1st 2018.{{Mediacat|imagecat=images by Carlos Ezquerra}} ==Star Wars bibliography== *''[[Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand]]'' *[[Boba Fett ½]] ==Sources== *''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' ==External links== *{{WP|Carlos Ezquerra}} {{Interlang |es=Carlos Ezquerra }} [[Category:Comic book pencillers]] [[Category:Comic book inkers]]
